subject: Geology
title: Silicate Minerals
description: A quick win: understand Silicate Minerals.

hook.text: One mineral family makes up over 90% of Earth's crust. Silicates run the planet, and most people never notice.

buildup.text: Silicate minerals are built from silicon and oxygen tetrahedra, the most abundant elements in the crust.

discovery.text: Quartz, feldspar, mica, and olivine are all silicates. They form granite, basalt, and most common rocks.

twist.text: Silicates link their tetrahedra in chains, sheets, and frameworks, creating wildly different minerals.

climax.text: Understanding silicates is understanding the crust itself. They are the building blocks of almost every rock.

punchline.text: Silicon and oxygen built the ground you stand on.

quiz.question: What percentage of Earth's crust do silicate minerals make up?
